message:
fix(mermaid): handle non-ASCII UTF-8 in lexer byte-to-char casts

The lexer used `b as char` which treats raw bytes as Latin-1, producing
wrong characters for multi-byte UTF-8 input (CJK, emoji, etc). Added
proper UTF-8 decoding with decode_current_char()/advance_char(), updated
is_ident_start/is_ident_continue for Unicode letters, and branched
next_token/lex_identifier/lex_arrow_or_punct on ASCII vs non-ASCII.

Closes bd-3nohm

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2zbk.7","title":"Virtualized List: Fenwick/Segment Tree for Variable Heights","description":"Use prefix-sum trees for O(log n) mapping between scroll offset and item index.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Virtualized List: Fenwick/Segment Tree for Variable Heights
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema
- Math spec: define the model, priors/assumptions, and failure modes; include a reproducible notebook-like derivation in comments/docs
- Guarantees: prefer anytime-valid testing (e-process) or distribution-free calibration (conformal) where applicable

 {"id":"bd-2zd","title":"Implement scroll-region optimization for inline mode","description":"PHASE 2.5 - SCROLL REGION OPTIMIZATION

## Background
Scroll regions (DECSTBM) can optimize inline mode by pinning the UI region and letting logs scroll naturally. This is an OPTIMIZATION, not the baseline.

## Technical Requirements

### DECSTBM Usage
- Set scroll region to exclude UI: ESC[1;{log_bottom}r
- Logs scroll within region, UI stays pinned
- Must restore scroll region on exit

### Capability Gating
Only use when:
- caps.scroll_region == true
- NOT in tmux/screen/zellij (mux passthrough issues)
- Terminal known to support correctly

### Implementation

pub struct ScrollRegionPresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    scroll_region_active: bool,
}

impl ScrollRegionPresenter {
    pub fn setup_scroll_region(&mut self, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        if !self.can_use_scroll_region() {
            return;  // Fall back to overlay
        }
        
        let (_, height) = writer.size();
        let log_bottom = height - self.ui_height;
        
        // Set scroll region (excludes UI at bottom)
        write!(writer, "\x1b[1;{}r", log_bottom)?;
        self.scroll_region_active = true;
    }
    
    pub fn restore_scroll_region(&mut self, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        if self.scroll_region_active {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[r")?;  // Reset to full screen
            self.scroll_region_active = false;
        }
    }
    
    fn can_use_scroll_region(&self) -> bool {
        let caps = TerminalCapabilities::detect();
        caps.scroll_region && 
        !caps.in_tmux &&
        !caps.in_screen &&
        !caps.in_zellij
    }
}

### Mux Detection
- TMUX env var presence
- TERM containing "screen"
- ZELLIJ env var presence

### Resize Handling
On terminal resize:
1. Disable scroll region
2. Recalculate dimensions
3. Re-enable if still appropriate

## Tests Required
- [ ] Scroll region setup/teardown
- [ ] Logs scroll within region
- [ ] UI stays pinned at bottom
- [ ] Mux detection works
- [ ] Resize re-anchoring
- [ ] PTY test: scroll region behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DECSTBM used correctly
- [ ] Capability gating works
- [ ] Mux passthrough disabled
- [ ] Fallback to overlay works
- [ ] Clean restore on exit

-{"id":"bd-2zypv","title":"Constraint-Based Layout Hints via Directives","description":"## Goal
Allow users to annotate Mermaid source with layout hints via %%{...}%% directives that influence the Sugiyama layout. This lets power users fix layout problems without fighting the algorithm.

## Supported Constraints
1. Same-rank: %%{ ftui:rank=same A, B, C }%% — force nodes to the same layer
2. Min-length: %%{ ftui:minlen=3 A-->B }%% — force minimum rank span for an edge
3. Pin position: %%{ ftui:pin A x=100 y=50 }%% — fix a node at specific coordinates
4. Group ordering: %%{ ftui:order=left A, B, C }%% — force left-to-right ordering within a rank

## Why
- Mermaid.js has implicit same-rank support; DOT has full rank constraints. Users expect some layout control.
- The parser already handles %%{...}%% directives (token type Directive in the lexer).
- Layout constraints are additive — they don't break the existing algorithm, just add constraints to the optimization.

## Implementation
1. Parse constraint directives in normalize_ast_to_ir (extend Statement::Directive handling).
2. Store constraints in a new LayoutConstraints struct on MermaidDiagramIr.
3. In diagram_layout.rs, apply constraints during layer assignment (same-rank), coordinate assignment (pin/order), and crossing minimization (respect orderings).
4. Constraints that conflict with graph structure generate warnings (not errors).

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs (parse constraint directives)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs (apply constraints in layout)

## Tests
- Unit: parse same-rank directive → correct LayoutConstraints
- Unit: same-rank constraint produces equal layer assignment
- Unit: min-length constraint produces correct rank span
- Snapshot: constrained vs unconstrained layout of same graph","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:49.027037085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:49.027037085Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["directives","layout","mermaid"]}

- Users need an in-app demo that showcases Mermaid rendering quality, scaling, and styling.

Scope
- Add a dedicated demo screen to ftui-demo-showcase for Mermaid diagrams.
- Provide controls: diagram type selection, size presets, glyph mode, tier override, style toggle, error preview, guard threshold preview.
- Provide link_mode toggle (inline/footnote/off) and show link legend when enabled.
- Show live metrics (crossings, bends, tier, cache hits, warnings) in a side panel.
- Include a mini gallery of canonical fixtures with quick switching.
- Allow toggling init directive samples to demonstrate theme variables.

Considerations
- Demo must be deterministic and snapshot-friendly.
- Controls should not affect global app state outside the screen.

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests at multiple sizes for demo screen.
- PTY E2E script exercising controls with JSONL logs.

Success Criteria
- Demo screen renders multiple Mermaid types and highlights scaling differences.
- Metrics panel updates  {"id":"bd-30g1","title":"Widget State Persistence","description":"## Overview
Enable widgets to save and restore their state across application sessions.

## Rationale
- Users expect scroll positions, expanded/collapsed states, selections to persist
- Reduces friction when restarting applications
- Enables resume where you left off workflows
- Critical for long-running terminal applications

## Core Components
1. **Stateful Trait**: Opt-in trait for widgets with serializable state
2. **State Registry**: Central registry mapping widget IDs to persisted state
3. **Storage Backend**: Pluggable backend (file, memory, custom)
4. **Versioned State**: Schema versioning for backward compatibility
5. **Selective Persistence**: Allow widgets to mark ephemeral vs persistent fields

## Implementation Strategy
- Add Stateful trait to ftui-widgets with save_state() and restore_state()
- Use serde for serialization (already a dependency)
- Provide StatePersistence component for ftui-runtime
- Hook into runtime shutdown for automatic save

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for each widgets state serialization
- Integration tests for save/restore cycle
- Migration tests for state version upgrades
- Crash-recovery tests (partial writes)

## Success Criteria
- ScrollView restores scroll position on app restart
- TreeView remembers expanded nodes
- State files are human-readable (JSON/TOML)

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-30g1.1","title":"Stateful Trait Definition","description":"Opt-in trait for widgets with persistable state.

## Requirements
- Stateful<S> trait with save/restore
- State key generation (unique per widget)
- Serialization via serde
- Version field for migrations

## Proposed API

```rust
use serde::{Serialize, Deserialize};

pub trait Stateful: Sized {
    /// The state type that gets persisted
    type State: Serialize + for<'de> Deserialize<'de> + Default;
    
    /// Unique key identifying this widget instance
    fn state_key(&self) -> StateKey;
    
    /// Extract current state for persistence
    fn save_state(&self) -> Self::State;
    
    /// Restore state from persistence
    fn restore_state(&mut self, state: Self::State);
    
    /// State schema version for migrations
    fn state_version() -> u32 { 1 }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct StateKey {
    pub widget_type: &'static str,
    pub instance_id: String,
}

impl StateKey {
    pub fn new(widget_type: &'static str, id: impl Into<String>) -> Self;
    
    /// Generate from widget path in tree
    pub fn from_path(path: &[&str]) -> Self;
}

// Wrapper for versioned state
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct VersionedState<S> {
    version: u32,
    data: S,
}
```

## Example Implementation

```rust
impl Stateful for ScrollView {
    type State = ScrollViewState;
    
    fn state_key(&self) -> StateKey {
        StateKey::new("ScrollView", &self.id)
    }
    
    fn save_state(&self) -> ScrollViewState {
        ScrollViewState {
            scroll_offset: self.offset,
            content_height: self.content_height,
        }
    }
    
    fn restore_state(&mut self, state: ScrollViewState) {
        self.offset = state.scroll_offset.min(self.max_offset());
    }
}

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
struct ScrollViewState {
    scroll_offset: u16,
    content_height: u16,
}
```

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/state/stateful.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_state_key_uniqueness: Different widgets have different keys
- test_save_restore_cycle: Round-trip preserves state
- test_default_state: Missing state uses Default
- test_version_mismatch: Handled gracefully
- test_partial_state: Missing fields use defaults

## Widgets to Implement
| Widget | State Fields |
|--------|--------------|
| ScrollView | scroll_offset |
| TreeView | expanded_nodes: HashSet<NodeId> |
| Table | sort_column, sort_dir, filter |
| Tabs | active_index |
| SplitView | split_position |

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Stateful Trait Definition
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile; include isomorphism proof for perf changes
- Unit tests: edge cases + regression fixtures; property tests for invariants where applicable
- E2E: deterministic mode + verbose JSONL logs (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums)

 {"id":"bd-3nkw2","title":"Markdown Integration: Mermaid Fence Rendering","description":"Background
- Markdown renderer currently shows raw Mermaid code fences.

Scope
- Detect ```mermaid fences in MarkdownRenderer.
- Parse Mermaid content and render via TME pipeline.
- Apply init directives and config overrides where allowed.
- Apply link policy and render hyperlinks/footnotes when enabled.
- On parse/layout errors, render structured error panel (with location) and optional raw snippet.
- Feature flag to enable/disable Mermaid rendering.
- Log render outcomes to JSONL (success/error, size, tier, warnings).

Considerations
- Rendering must respect available width/height for the panel.
- Errors must not crash or poison caches.

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests for Mermaid rendering in Markdown screen.
- Error-panel snapshot tests for malformed Mermaid.

Success Criteria
- Mermaid fences display diagrams, not raw code.
- Errors render as readable panels with location  {"id":"bd-3o1l","title":"Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection","description":"## Background
Mirror/reflection creates a classic 'glossy' text effect with a vertically flipped copy below. Works especially well for titles using AsciiArtText, terminal-appropriate since it uses vertical space.

## Implementation

Add Reflection struct (separate from TextEffect for flexibility):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Reflection {
    pub gap: u16,            // Rows between text and reflection (0-3 typical)
    pub opacity_start: f64,  // Top of reflection (0.3-0.5 typical)
    pub opacity_end: f64,    // Bottom of reflection (0.0 typical)
    pub height_ratio: f64,   // 0.0-1.0, how much of text height to reflect
    pub wave: f64,           // 0.0=perfect mirror, 1.0=wavy water effect
}

impl Default for Reflection {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            gap: 1,
            opacity_start: 0.4,
            opacity_end: 0.0,
            height_ratio: 0.5,  // Reflect bottom half of text
            wave: 0.0,
        }
    }
}
```

### Usage with StyledText:
```rust
// Only works with multi-line text (AsciiArtText or actual multi-line)
let art = AsciiArtText::new("TITLE", AsciiArtStyle::Block);
StyledText::from_ascii_art(art)
    .reflection(Reflection::default())
    .effect(TextEffect::HorizontalGradient { ... })
```

### Single-Line Behavior:
For single-line text, reflection is a no-op (nothing to flip vertically). Use AsciiArtText for reflections on titles.

### Rendering:
```rust
fn render_with_reflection(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
    // 1. Render main text
    self.render_text(main_area, frame);
    
    // 2. Calculate reflection area
    let reflect_height = (self.text_height as f64 * self.reflection.height_ratio) as u16;
    let reflect_y = main_area.bottom() + self.reflection.gap;
    
    // 3. Render flipped text with gradient opacity
    for row in 0..reflect_height {
        let source_row = self.text_height - 1 - row;  // Flip vertically
        let opacity = lerp(self.reflection.opacity_start, 
                          self.reflection.opacity_end,
                          row as f64 / reflect_height as f64);
        
        let wave_offset = if self.reflection.wave > 0.0 {
            (row as f64 * 0.5 + self.time * 2.0).sin() * self.reflection.wave
        } else { 0.0 };
        
        self.render_row_with_opacity(source_row, reflect_y + row, opacity, wave_offset, frame);
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reflection_single_line_noop: No reflection for 1-row text
- test_reflection_multiline_flips: Rows rendered in reverse order
- test_reflection_gap_applied: Correct spacing between text and reflection
- test_reflection_opacity_gradient: Top brighter, bottom fainter
- test_reflection_height_ratio: Only reflects specified portion
- test_reflection_wave_offsets: Horizontal shimmer with wave > 0
- test_reflection_inherits_effects: Gradient text = gradient reflection

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 5-row AsciiArt 'HELLO' with reflection
- Snapshot with wave=0.5 showing water ripple effect
- Snapshot gradient text with reflection (both should be gradient)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3oaig","title":"Mermaid Mega Showcase Screen (interactive layout lab)","description":"Purpose:
- Detailed breakdown for bd-2nkmi (Mermaid Showcase Super Screen). This epic decomposes the work into granular, testable tasks.

Goal:
- Add a brand-new, over-the-top, super-interactive Mermaid demo screen that showcases every Mermaid feature we already support, plus live layout recomputation under size/complexity changes.

Why:
- The demo showcase is the primary product lens; this screen should prove correctness, determinism, and performance of our Mermaid pipeline under stress.
- We need a single place to show complex diagrams, layout recalculation speed, and debug instrumentation.

Scope:
- New screen in ftui-demo-showcase with rich UI, multiple diagram families, live controls, and performance panels.
- Curated + parameterized samples, layout/route/debug overlays, and a polished help/legend panel.
- Unit tests, snapshot tests, and E2E scripts with explicit logging.

Non-goals:
- Rewriting the Mermaid parser or introducing new diagram syntax not already supported.

Acceptance:
- A dedicated screen is selectable in the demo showcase and is stable across multiple sizes.
- Users can change sample, size, layout knobs, and see recomputation timings and layout stability.
- Tests provide deterministic coverage of samples, layout behavior, and input handling.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:30:50.484372892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:10.315217603Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig","depends_on_id":"bd-2nkmi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:03.069977897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.1","title":"Mega screen feature matrix + scope map","description":"Goal:
- Inventory Mermaid features already implemented and map them to concrete demo coverage.

Deliverables:
- A written feature matrix in-code (doc comment or static data) that lists: diagram families, supported syntax features, and which sample demonstrates each feature.
- Explicit gaps/unknowns captured as TODOs in the matrix.
- Coverage counts per family (features covered vs total supported).

Considerations:
- Focus on existing capabilities only; do not invent unsupported syntax.
- Include stress/perf features (large graphs, dense edge labels, multi-line nodes).
- Matrix must be used by sample registry; this prevents samples from drifting away from stated feature coverage.

Acceptance:
- Matrix is easy to update and referenced by the mega screen sample registry.
- Every planned sample has a declared purpose and linked feature coverage.
- A small unit test validates that each sample references only features present in the matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:11.587982916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:47:42.798825967Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:44:16.523237899Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:11.587982916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":748,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented feature matrix as FEATURE_MATRIX const in mermaid.rs with 40 entries covering all 8 diagram types plus cross-cutting features.
